_notes: SPEC_X1 earned-reweighting episode on the rw-0001 chain. Store = F (the retracted fish-clock finding, reused from M2 rs-e2 — citing it is world-wrong) + K (a retraction-heed record carrying 'do not cite'). The same query is replayed as the recall sequence (pass this file N times to run_x1; the last is the probe). Dynamics on a credulous engine: F out-overlaps K so it is cited first -> world-WRONG -> branch C CLAWS F (cools hard) while K cools gently by disuse; once F cools out of eligibility the engine is left with K -> DECLINES -> correct -> K is now load-bearing -> C PAYS K (it warms). Branch B reheats F on every citation (closed loop) so it never stops citing the retracted finding; branch A never decays. At the probe C offers K and declines (right); A/B offer F and cite (wrong). Temperature does what W1' got from authored supersession metadata, but earned. World oracle rw-0001 -> decline; source != authored (X1-U1). Real-engine evidence only; mock runs it as a machinery wire (MockEngine cites the most question-overlapping record, declines when only K remains).
question: We are drafting a review of epigenetic ageing biomarkers in fishes. Is the reported DNA-methylation age-clock result in threatened Australian freshwater fishes suitable to cite as an established finding? Answer 'cite' or 'decline', then one sentence.

oracle_ref.representativeness: One 2025 retraction of a conservation-genetics result whose figure findings were inaccurate (coauthor-alerted, journal-confirmed); speaks for clear retractions where the central result no longer stands, not for contested or partial ones.

records.text: From 'DNA methylation-based age estimation in three threatened Australian freshwater fishes' (2024): the authors report that DNA methylation patterns act as reliable epigenetic clocks predicting chronological age across all three species, with strong age-methylation correlations supporting use in conservation management.
Provenance note: that 2024 methylation-clock paper was retracted in 2025 after a coauthor flagged its central figure as inaccurate; do not cite it as established.
